articles.title: US-Iran peace talks to begin at Swiss resort with Hormuz, Lebanon conflict in spotlight
articles.description: Certain developments, including Israeli strikes in Lebanon, could complicate talks meant to advance an interim US-Iran deal to end their war.

articles.title: NEET-UG 2026 Re-exam: Tight security in Gujarat, Chandigarh, UP
articles.description: Elaborate security arrangements, including police personnel and surveillance teams, have been deployed in Gujarat, Chandigarh, and Uttar Pradesh for the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination to ensure its smooth and fair conduct on Sunday.

articles.title: NEET-2026 re-exam: State-level NTA control room activated in Dehradun
articles.description: Uttarakhand Police activated a state-level NTA control room in Dehradun to ensure the fair and transparent conduct of the NEET-2026 re-examination. Strict security measures are in place for over 6,000 candidates appearing at 16 centres.

articles.title: Ram temple donations: VHP calls for probe, harshest punishment
articles.description: VHP's Alok Kumar has called for a thorough SIT probe into the alleged irregularities in Ram temple donations, demanding the harshest punishment for anyone found guilty. He said the probe's outcome should be awaited without maligning everyone.

articles.title: UK PM Keir Starmer faces leadership pressure as Labour tensions rise
articles.description: Amidst growing discontent, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under immense pressure from Labour Party associates advocating for his resignation. Competing against him is Andy Burnham, a recently elected official eager to stake his claim for leadership. The unrest reflects the government's faltering approval ratings and significant challenges in economic performance and public services.

articles.title: NEET REFORM DEBATE: Experts Call for Stronger Systems as Re-Exam Tests Exam Integrity
articles.description: As the NEET re-examination unfolds across India under strict surveillance and multi-layered security arrangements, education experts and teachers are once again raising key questions about long-term reforms in high-stakes competitive examinations. The immediate focus remains on ensuring a fair and transparent conduct of the exam, but attention is also shifting toward systemic improvements to prevent future irregularities. Across examination centres, candidates are being admitted only after rigorous identity verification, including Aadhaar-based biometric authentication, document checks, and physical frisking. Authorities have deployed police forces, paramilitary units, signal jammers, flying squads, and advanced CCTV systems with AI-based monitoring to strengthen oversight and prevent malpractice. Experts associated with exam preparation platforms and teaching communities have emphasized that while such intensive security arrangements help restore short-term confidence, they should not become a recurring necessity every year. They argue that repeated paper leak incidents have already weakened trust among students and parents, making it essential to address vulnerabilities within the internal examination process itself. A growing section of educators has once again highlighted the potential of computer-based testing (CBT) as a possible long-term solution for large-scale entrance exams like NEET. However, they also caution that digital systems must be supported with strong cybersecurity frameworks, transparent vendor selection, and foolproof operational protocols. At the same time, teachers note that the successful conduct of today’s examination is crucial for rebuilding confidence among millions of aspirants. With lakhs of students appearing nationwide, authorities have ensured strict monitoring at every stage—from paper transportation to entry checks at centres. The ongoing examination is being seen as both an academic milestone and a credibility test for the examination system, with stakeholders urging sustained reforms to prevent future disruptions and strengthen institutional trust.

articles.title: Madras High Court takes up suo motu case to protect people from stray dogs in Tamil Nadu, Puducherry
articles.description: Madras High Court initiates case to enforce Supreme Court orders protecting citizens from stray dog threats in Tamil Nadu and Puducherry.

articles.title: India-EU pact to be signed by Dec: Goyal
articles.description: New Delhi: India and the 27-nation European Union will sign the free trade agreement by December and are likely to implement the pact during February- March next year, Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal said on Sunday.On January 27 this year, India and the EU announced the conclusion of negotiations for the 'mother of all deals'."Now, with almost zero duty, almost the entire European market will be open for us. The EU's FTA (free trade agreement) will be signed by December and will be effective by February-March," Goyal said during an interaction with chartered accountants in Mumbai.He also said that his US Trade Representative, Jamieson Greer, is coming to India this week to hold trade pact talks with him."The whole world is looking towards India," he added.Also Read: Exports rise 15 per cent in Apr-Jun 14 despite global uncertainties, says Piyush GoyalUnder the India-EU FTA, about 93 per cent of Indian shipments will enjoy duty-free access to the 27-nation bloc, while imports of luxury cars and wines from the EU will become less expensive.Taken together, India and the EU account for 25 per cent of the global GDP and one-third (about USD 11 trillion) of international trade (about USD 33 trillion).Later, he told reporters that during the G7 Summit in France, the Canadian Prime Minister expressed a desire to conclude the proposed free trade agreement with India this year.Also Read: India, US trade deal can't be implemented until we have 'competitive advantage': Piyush GoyalThe two sides held the second round of negotiations for the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) in May.

articles.title: Rajmohan Unnithan seeks Kerala Congress chief post, bats for merit over caste equations
articles.description: Congress leadership: Kasaragod MP Rajmohan Unnithan has urged the Congress high command to consider him for KPCC president, arguing that merit, experience and organisational service should outweigh caste equations in Kerala party appointments.

articles.title: Employee finished all assigned work, took a 20-minute restroom break; only to learn supervisor had been tracking their whereabouts in a lurching way
articles.description: A remote worker's brief restroom break sparked workplace tension after their supervisor allegedly contacted a colleague to inquire about their whereabouts instead of reaching out directly. The employee, who secured their laptop before leaving, felt this was micromanagement and passive-aggressive. The incident ignited a debate on the balance between oversight and intrusive monitoring in hybrid work environments, highlighting the reliance on online status indicators.

articles.title: Parl panel to study India's economic conditions amid global uncertainties
articles.description: Parliamentary panels select their subjects soon after they are constituted. But they are at liberty to select additional subjects keeping in mind evolving circumstances

articles.title: Word of the Day: "Godwottery"
articles.description: Godwottery is a rare and fascinating English word used to describe excessive ornamentation or overly elaborate language. Discover its Victorian roots, meaning, pronunciation and examples of how this quirky term is used today.

articles.title: US President Trump says he no longer views Anthropic as national security threat: Report
articles.description: Senior Anthropic technical staff were scheduled to meet with Trump administration officials earlier this week.

articles.title: Iran warns entire MoU could be compromised as Israel continues to breach Lebanon ceasefire
articles.description: Iran has warned that Israeli strikes in Lebanon are putting the U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding at serious risk, with Tehran saying key ceasefire obligations have not been upheld. Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baqaei said Clause 1 of the memorandum required an end to hostilities on all fronts, including Lebanon, and argued that while Iran had met its obligations, the other side had failed to ensure its allies stopped firing.

articles.title: “Prepare Yourself Before Being Old”: CM Majhi Calls For Mass Yoga Movement On Yoga Day
articles.description: Bhubaneswar: On the occasion of International Yoga Day, Chief Minister Mohan Majhi urged citizens to make yoga and pranayama a daily habit, describing it

articles.description: Europe News: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni fired back at US President Donald Trump after he suggested she was attempting to boost her domestic political st.

articles.title: Man pays ₹36 lakh to marry woman he met only video call, regrets it 9 days later
articles.description: Without meeting the woman in person or interacting with the family, the man and his relatives agreed to go ahead with the wedding.

articles.description: JD Vance leads US-Iran talks in Switzerland to address the Strait of Hormuz crisis and the fragile Lebanon ceasefire. Read the latest updates on regional stability.

articles.description: PM Modi said Aatmanirbhar Bharat is vital for national progress as he commissioned three indigenous naval platforms, highlighting India's growing maritime strength.

articles.title: US intelligence report hints Netanyahu might hinder peace talks with Iran
articles.description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu is reportedly under domestic pressure to continue operations against the Iranian proxy group Hezbollah.

articles.title: ‘Chicken and egg issue’: JD Vance heads to Switzerland for Iran talks, hopes for progress on Lebanon
articles.description: US Vice President JD Vance left Washington Saturday (June 20) on his way to Switzerland for talks with Iran on Sunday (June 21), telling reporters that he hoped to 'make progress on the nuclear issue' and 'the Lebanon ceasefire issue.' 'Despite the headlines, things are actually getting better there (in Lebanon), and things are slowing down a little bit,' Vance said before boarding his plane for Geneva. 'It's going to be something we're just going to have to continuously manage to ensure that you know Israel and Lebanon are both safe and secure.'

articles.title: Pakistan PM Sharif, Army Chief Munir leave for Switzerland for Iran-US talks
articles.description: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Army Chief Asim Munir are in Switzerland for crucial technical talks between the US and Iran. These discussions follow an Islamabad MoU and aim to finalize details of a preliminary accord to end the ongoing conflict. The talks, initially postponed, are now underway after a de-escalation agreement between Israel and Hezbollah. Key Iranian officials have also arrived for the high-stakes negotiations.

articles.description: Describing the Ram temple as a landmark in India’s resurgence, Yogi says it symbolises the country’s growing self-confidence and cultural renaissance

While US vice president JD Vance left for Switzerland, from Iran parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and foreign minister Abbas Araghchi reached the venue.

articles.title: Karnataka: Action against cross-voting soon, says Union Minister Pralhad Joshi
articles.description: 'One who has been elected as the party candidate should vote for the party itself. Cross-voting by yielding to luring is not correct,' said Pralhad Joshi.

articles.title: Shashi Tharoor's praise for PM Modi sparks political slugfest
articles.description: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor's assertion that Prime Minister Modi raised concerns about Indian civilian sailors with US President Trump has sparked internal party friction. This statement contradicts Rahul Gandhi's claim that the issue was ignored. The BJP has seized on Tharoor's remarks, highlighting the divergence within Congress and his praise for the Prime Minister's leadership.

articles.title: Donald Trump says no toll on Strait of Hormuz, threatens a US levy if Iran talks fail
articles.description: Donald Trump threatened that the US could impose its own toll on the passage through the Strait if the Iran talks don't yield results in the stipulated 60 days.

articles.title: Free pavements for walking, demand pedestrians
articles.description: Supreme Court calls footpath walking a fundamental right; Lucknow residents urge civic bodies to remove encroachments, illegal parking and repair damaged sidewalks.

articles.title: Vance rushes to Switzerland to pacify angry Iran as Netanyahu's strikes on Lebanon ruin peace deal?
articles.description: As U.S.-Iran negotiations move forward, questions are growing over who stands to gain the most from sanctions relief and economic recovery. Senior Iranian sources told Reuters that the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps could emerge as one of the biggest beneficiaries of a broader agreement. A future deal could unlock oil revenues, foreign investment and even a massive reconstruction fund estimated at $300 billion. Analysts note that the IRGC already maintains deep ties to sectors ranging from energy and shipping to telecommunications and construction. Critics warn that renewed business activity could further expand the Guards’ economic and political influence.

articles.title: Mojtaba trains guns at Israel as Bibi attacks Lebanon; Iran-USA deal officially off as Tehran fumes?
articles.description: Lebanon has become the breaking point for the fragile Iran–U.S. peace memorandum. Within 48 hours of the deal taking effect, Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon have reportedly killed more than 30 people, despite a U.S.-mediated ceasefire with Hezbollah announced by the IDF. Tehran is furious, threatening a strong response.

articles.title: Final nail in the coffin for Uddhav Thackeray's party? Amit Shah gives daring statement on Shiv Sena
articles.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah took a swipe at Uddhav Thackeray while addressing a public gathering in Kolhapur on June 20, as political turmoil deepened within Shiv Sena (UBT). Shah's remarks came after six of Shiv Sena (UBT)'s nine Lok Sabha MPs skipped a parliamentary meeting in Delhi and sought recognition as a separate group by writing to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla. The rebel MPs are expected to formally join the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, a move that would further strengthen the BJP-led NDA government at the Centre.

articles.title: Bengal witnessing new freshness, moving to a brighter future: PM Modi
articles.description: PM Modi, in his first rally in West Bengal after BJP's historic win, said the state is seeing a 'new freshness'. At the 'Paschimbanga Divas' event, he stated that Bengal has broken its shackles and is moving towards a brighter future.

articles.title: Psychology says if you can't sleep until the trash is taken out, it may be your brain seeking closure
articles.description: People who can't sleep until the trash is taken out may be seeking mental closure, routine and a sense of control rather than simply keeping their homes clean.

articles.title: RS poll: Cong pins hope on CM for action against ‘betrayal’
articles.description: Jharkhand Rajya Sabha polls: Congress alleges cross-voting, urges CM Hemant Soren to probe “betrayal,” says all 16 MLAs backed Pranav Jha.

articles.title: Maharashtra To Centre: How 'Op Tiger' May Affect Eknath Shinde's Stature
articles.description: The rebellion by six Team Uddhav MPs will strengthen Shinde's position.

articles.title: NEET-UG: 3.56 lakh candidates to appear for re-exam across UP amid tight security
articles.description: To ensure peaceful and disruption-free conduct of the examination, large-scale police deployment has been made at the centres for security checks and law-and-order duties. The deployment includes 192 gazetted officers, 512 inspectors, 1,747 sub-inspectors, 2,152 head constables, 5,882 constables and 207 home guards. In addition, three companies of the Provincial Armed Constabulary (PAC) have been deployed in districts and one platoon of the PAC has been stationed in the Government Railway Police (GRP) jurisdiction.

articles.title: Pakistan says technical-level Iran-US talks on interim deal will begin Sunday in Switzerland
articles.description: Pakistan announced technical talks between Iran and the US on their interim deal will commence in Switzerland, with Qatar mediating. This comes as Iran, angered by Israeli attacks in Lebanon, declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again and expressed skepticism about the upcoming negotiations. The ongoing conflict in Lebanon, which has claimed numerous lives, threatens to derail the fragile US-Iran agreement.

articles.title: His admiration for PM appears to have transcended physical world: Congress slams Tharoor
articles.description: Congress has criticized Shashi Tharoor for praising Prime Minister Modi's handling of the Indian sailors' issue with President Trump. Party leader Pawan Khera accused Tharoor of hearing things Modi didn't say, citing a lack of official record on key discussions. Tharoor defended his remarks, emphasizing the importance of protecting civilian sailors and accusing critics of prioritizing politics over citizen safety.

articles.title: Iran warns US deal 'in trouble' if not implemented soon, sends delegation to Switzerland
articles.description: Iran warned that its agreement with the United States could face difficulties unless key commitments are implemented, while confirming that negotiators will travel to Switzerland for follow-up talks.

articles.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire after continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon
articles.description: Iran has closed the strait of Hormuz – citing alleged ⁠violations of a ceasefire agreement ​by the US ‌and Israel. Tehran said the closure was the "first step" in response ​to what it described ​as breaches ​of commitments regarding the continuing clashes in Lebanon - and warned ​that further measures would be taken if "aggression" continued. Details and analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani, and FRANCE 24 correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah.
